stem: A company's revenue increased from $200,000 last year to $230,000 this year. What is the percent increase in revenue?

distractorRationale.A: Student divided the increase by the new amount instead of the original: $30,000 / $230,000 ≈ 0.13, so 13%.
distractorRationale.C: Student used $30,000 directly as the percentage without dividing by the base: reported 30%.
distractorRationale.D: Student divided the original by the new amount: $200,000 / $230,000 ≈ 0.87, so 87%.
